q: The attractive forces that hold atoms together in compounds are also known as _______.
According to _______, no more two electrons in an atom can have same orbital.
According to the Aufbau principle, the electron occupies that sub-shell with the _______ energy.
All electrons in doubly occupied orbitals have the _______ spin.
No more than two electrons can occupy the same orbital and must have _______ spins.
The electrons in the valence shells (outermost shells) of an atom are known as the _______.
The octet rule does not apply to _______ elements.
The strength of sharing or transferring the valence electrons depends on the _______ of the atoms involved in the chemical bonding.
_______ is a measure of the ability of an atom to attract the shared electrons to itself.
The amount of energy required to _______ an electron from a gaseous atom.
The elements having high electron affinity easily gain electrons, resulting in the formation of the _______.
The elements having low ionisation energy easily lose electrons, resulting in the formation of the _______.
The higher the first ionisation energy of an element, the _______ is its electronegativity.
The higher the value of the electronegativity, the _______ is the atom's attraction to electrons.
The type of chemical bonding depends on the types of _______ that are involved.
The electronic structure of Cl- is the same as _______.
An ionic bond is formed by _______ of electrons between two atoms.
Ionic bonding depends on the difference in _______ between two atoms.
Ionic bonding takes place when the difference in electronegativity is greater than _______.
Ionic bonding occurs between reactive metals and reactive _______.
A metal loses electrons to form _______.
The non-metal atom readily gains electrons to form _______.
The greater the charge on the ions, the _______ electrostatic attraction.
The greater the charge on the ions, the _______ ionic bond.
In ionic compounds, the cations and anions are held together by strong _______.
In solid ionic compounds, their ions are held in _______ positions and cannot move.
The crystalline structure of an ionic solid is called a _______.
NaCl crystal has a _______ structure.
The term _______ is used to express the number of ions surrounding the central ion in the lattice.
The coordination ratio of Na+ ion to Cl- ion in NaCl crystalline structure is _______.
The simple ratio of Na+ ion to Cl- ion in NaCl crystalline structure is _______.
Ionic compounds are compounds that consist of _______.
_______ is the smallest repeating unit of a crystal lattice.
A crystal lattice that shows the _______ pattern of the entire crystal.
Simple cubic unit cell is a cubic cell with an atom in each _______ of the cube.
Face-centred cubic unit cell is a cubic cell with an atom on each corner, and an atom at the _______ of each face.
_______ unit cell is a cubic cell with an atom in each corner of the cube.
_______ cubic unit cell is a cubic cell with an atom on each corner and another in the centre of the cube.
_______ cubic unit cell is a cubic cell with an atom on each corner and another in the centre of the cube.
Covalent bonds are formed when electrons are _______.
An optimum distance between two nuclei is called the _______.
The _______ valence electrons in each of the bonding atoms are involved in covalent bond.
In covalent bonding, the _______ valence electrons are not involved in bonding.
The higher the number of bonded electrons, the _______ the attraction between the two atoms.
The higher the number of bonded electrons, the _______ the bond length.
A triple bond is formed by sharing _______ pairs of electrons.
The length of the bond is determined by the number of _______ electrons.
The bond length of the single bond is the _______.
A coordinate bond, or dative bond, is a special type of _______ bond.
The ammonia molecule possesses a _______ of electrons on the nitrogen atom.
A water (H2O) molecule that possesses _______ lone pair electrons on the oxygen atom.
The central atom in an electron-deficient molecule has valence electrons of _______ than 8.
In an expanded valence shell molecule has valence electrons of more than _______.
The VSEPR theory is used to predict the _______ of covalent molecules.
In BeCl2, there are _______ bonding pairs without lone pairs.
The molecular shape of BeCl2 is _______.
In the CO2 molecule, there are _______ bonding pairs without lone pairs.
The shape of CO2 according to VSEPR theory is _______.
BF3 has a _______ shape.
The bond angle of F-B-F in BF3 molecule is _______.
The shape of the molecule of NH3 is _______ by VSEPR model.
The shape of CH4 according to VSEPR model is _______.
The shape of water is _______.
The angle between two bonds in a linear molecule is _______.
In some substances in solids, atoms are joined together by single covalent bonds, exciting _______ structures.
A graphite has a layer and _______ structure.
In a graphite, each carbon atom joins to the other _______ carbon atoms by covalent bonds.
Each carbon bonded layer is formed with _______ rings of carbon atoms, in graphite.
In a graphite, there are weak van der Waals forces between the layers, which give rise to a _______ structure.
Graphite has _______ electrons in each layer.
Diamond and graphite are _______ molecules.
Fullerene (C60), carbon nanotubes, and graphene do not exist as _______ structures.
Fullerene (C60), carbon nanotubes, and graphene are _______ covalent molecules.
The fullerene (C60), carbon nanotubes, and graphene are allotropes of _______.
Silicon(IV) oxide has a similar structure to a _______.
Silica does not conduct electricity because it has no free moving _______.
In quartz, each silicon atom is bonded to _______ oxygen atoms.
In quartz, each oxygen atom is bonded to _______ silicon atoms.
Molecular shape is the _______ arrangement of the atoms that constitute a molecule.
Molecular orbital theory is a method for describing the electronic structure of molecules based on _______ energy levels.
Bond angle is an angle between any two bonds that include a common atom, usually measured in _______.
The forces of attraction and repulsion between interaction molecules are known as _______.
A covalent bond formed is non-polar if the two atoms have _______ electronegativity.
A covalent bond formed is polar if the two atoms have different _______.
When atoms share an unequal number of electrons between two atoms, a _______ covalent bond is formed.
Dipole moment is the measure of _______ in a molecule.
The degree of bond polarity is measured as a _______ of a bond.
_______ is a pair of equal in magnitude and opposite charges separated by a distance.
When atoms share an equal number of electrons, a _______ covalent bond is formed.
The direction of the dipole can also be shown by the sign _______.
_______ are weak intermolecular forces that bind molecules together.
London dispersion force is also called _______ interaction.
Dipole-Dipole forces are _______ than London dispersion forces.
Dipole-Dipole forces are _______ than ion-dipole interactions.
A hydrogen bond is a special type of _______ interaction that occurs between polar molecules.
Van der Waals forces are _______ forces.
The hydrogen bond is _______ than a covalent bond.
The hydrogen bond is the _______ of all intermolecular forces.
In metallic bond, the positively charged metallic nuclei are surrounded by _______ electrons.
The strength of metallic bond is _______ than that of an ionic bond.
